summary: Audience‑sourced recording captures an energetic Richmond show with standout Hell in a Bucket, Truckin', Good Lovin' and a smoking Crazy Fingers jam.
review: Both reviewers rave about the performance – a quick‑pacing first set that rolls into powerful jams, highlighted by Hell in a Bucket, Truckin', Good Lovin' and an especially fiery Playing > Crazy Fingers sequence. The audience recording is praised as clean and well balanced, letting the drums, space and guitar work shine.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise (70% weight) and a consistently good audience recording (30% weight) from two enthusiastic reviews yield an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the audio as "very nice" with clear drum and space echoes; no major drop‑outs or muddiness are mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as quick and sharp, second half builds into strong jams (Playing > Crazy Fingers, drums > space) with good crowd interaction.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Liquid guitar on the Playing > Crazy Fingers sequence praised.
band_member_comments.Phil: "I know the lie I've been living's no good" line highlighted; noted cooking with drummers.
band_member_comments.Bob: Whammy‑bar work on Crazy Fingers and vocal quote "Ya gotta punch back the night!" appreciated.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum and space sections receive special praise for echo, power and overall groove.
